title: scan (deprecated)
text: scan (deprecated) Scans a field or variable for a specified profile and returns the starting position of the profile. Note: The scan statement is deprecated. It has been superseded by $scan function, which is applicable to all component types. scan String , Profile Example: scan vString ,"house" Parameters Table 1. Parameters Parameter Data Type Description String String Field or variable to be scanned. If String is a Date, Time, or Datetime field, Uniface first converts the value to a string according to the default format from the language setup before performing the scan. Profile String String or syntax string, with a maximum length of 256 characters, that contains the profile to be matched. A syntax string is enclosed in single quotation marks ( ' ' ) or using $syntax . Return Values scan has no effect on $status . Table 2. Values returned in $result Value Meaning >0 Starting position in String of the first match. 0 Profile not found or String is an empty string (""). Use Allowed in all component types. Description The scan statement returns the position of the first character of String that matches the Profile provided. (The first character of a string is at position 1.) If Profile is a normal string, scan looks for an exact, case-sensitive match of Profile in String . If Profile is a syntax string, the match is not case sensitive. Lowercase is seen as both uppercase and lowercase, and uppercase is seen as uppercase only. You can use single quotes or $syntax to specify a syntax string. Use $syntax to perform case-insensitive scans when the string contains diacritics (for example an umlaut). If character attributes (bold, underline, and so on) are specified in the profile string, Uniface ignores them. In this case, abc (bold) is the same as abc (not bold). The asterisk wildcard ( * ) in Uniface stands for 0- n characters, so spaces also qualify in the syntax check. However, one asterisk in the syntax string causes Uniface to check only the following 32 characters. Example: Using scan The following examples show different ways of using the scan statement: vString = "Nibble1" NAME = "Myrtle" scan vString ,"bbl" ; $result = 3 scan vString ,"house" ; $result = 0 (string not found) scan vString ,'#' ; $result = 7 (first number in string) scan NAME,"yr*e" ; $result = 0 ; (syntax strings must be in single quotes) scan NAME,'yr*e' ; $result = 2 (syntax strings allowed) scan vString , NAME[5,6] ; $result = 5 (scan vString , "le") vString2 ="schön" scan vString2 , $syntax("hö*") ; $result=3 Copy Related Links length (deprecated) Return the number of characters in the specified string. $syntax Convert a string to a syntax string. $scan Returns the position of a substring within a string.
